meaning: adj.不能制胜的,不能克服的
examples: In a similar sense, the ever increasing number of online conversations may be disrupted by misinterpretations and anxiety that are produced by insuperable delays in the Internet connection.
But not, I think, an insuperable one.
That, and his age, may be insuperable handicaps.
Once the firm had achieved a position of strength, would-be competitors faced insuperable hurdles.
Given the disappearance of the budget surplus, this might seem insuperable, but there is a silver lining.
